comment_text.sn35.118:1.1: A long discourse of this name is at DN 21; see too the next discourse.

root_text.sn35.118:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.sn35.118:1.2: Atha kho sakko devānamindo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho sakko devānamindo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.118:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme no parinibbāyanti?
root_text.sn35.118:1.4: Ko pana, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyantī”ti?
root_text.sn35.118:2.1: “Santi kho, devānaminda, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.118:2.2: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.118:2.3: Tassa taṁ abhinandato abhivadato ajjhosāya tiṭṭhato tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.118:3.2: santi kho, devānaminda, manoviññeyyā dhammā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.118:3.3: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.118:3.4: Tassa taṁ abhinandato abhivadato ajjhosāya tiṭṭhato tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.118:3.6: Ayaṁ kho, devānaminda, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme no parinibbāyanti.
root_text.sn35.118:4.1: Santi ca kho, devānaminda, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.118:4.2: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.118:4.3: Tassa taṁ anabhinandato anabhivadato anajjhosāya tiṭṭhato na tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti, na tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.118:5.2: santi kho, devānaminda, manoviññeyyā dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.118:5.3: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn35.118:5.4: Tassa taṁ anabhinandato anabhivadato anajjhosāya tiṭṭhato na tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti na tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.118:5.6: Ayaṁ kho, devānaminda, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyantī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.118:0.2: 12. The World and the Kinds of Sensual Stimulation
translation_text.sn35.118:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.sn35.118:1.2: And then Sakka, lord of gods, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn35.118:1.3: “What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some sentient beings are not fully extinguished in this very life?
translation_text.sn35.118:1.4: What is the cause, what is the reason why some sentient beings are fully extinguished in this very life?”
translation_text.sn35.118:2.1: “Lord of gods, there are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.118:2.2: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keeps clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.118:2.3: their consciousness has that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.118:2.4: A mendicant with fuel for grasping does not become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.118:3.1: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.118:3.2: ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.118:3.3: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keeps clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.118:3.4: their consciousness has that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.118:3.5: A mendicant with fuel for grasping does not become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.118:3.6: That’s the cause, that’s the reason why some sentient beings are not fully extinguished in this very life.
translation_text.sn35.118:4.1: There are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.118:4.2: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.118:4.3: their consciousness doesn’t have that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.118:4.4: A mendicant free of grasping becomes extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.118:5.1: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.118:5.2: ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.118:5.3: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them,
translation_text.sn35.118:5.4: their consciousness doesn’t have that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.118:5.5: A mendicant free of grasping becomes extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.118:5.6: That’s the cause, that’s the reason why some sentient beings are fully extinguished in this very life.”
